description: A vertical, portrait-orientation slogan graphic from USCIS built on a full-color photograph of an outdoor immigration arrest. In the foreground, an older man with grey hair and beard, wearing a dark short-sleeve shirt, stands with his hands cuffed behind his back; to his right a law-enforcement officer is shown from behind wearing a green/camouflage tactical vest with patches reading 'POLICE' and 'ICE,' khaki tactical pants, and a holstered sidearm. The setting is a grassy area with a chain-link fence and green trees. A solid dark-blue rectangle in the left-center contains the slogan in bold white serif text on three lines: 'Making America Safe Again.' This is the same recurring DHS/USCIS branding template applied to enforcement photos. The messaging technique juxtaposes a handcuffed individual being arrested by ICE with the 'Making America Safe Again' slogan (echoing Trump's MAGA branding) to frame immigration enforcement as removing dangerous criminals and protecting the public. Target audience is the general public and Trump supporters, projecting strength on crime and immigration.
